maml:description.maml:para: Adds a new snippet from an existing file or pipeline input.

Imports content into PSSnips from two sources: File - reads a file from disk via -Path. The language is inferred from the source file's extension when -Language is not specified. Pipeline - collects lines piped into the function and joins them with newlines. Requires -Language when the extension cannot be inferred from context. The snippet file is written to the configured SnippetsDir and registered in the index. Use -Force to overwrite an existing snippet with the same name.
Mandatory. The identifier for the new snippet (no spaces, no extension).
Optional. Short description stored in the index.
Optional switch. Overwrites an existing snippet with the same name without prompting.
Optional (Pipe parameter set). Accepts string lines from the pipeline. All lines are collected and joined before saving.
Optional. Overrides the inferred or default language/extension.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. Array of tag strings for the snippet.
Mandatory. The identifier for the new snippet (no spaces, no extension).
Optional (File parameter set). Path to the source file to import. The language is derived from the file extension when -Language is omitted.
Optional. Short description stored in the index.
Optional switch. Overwrites an existing snippet with the same name without prompting.
Optional. Overrides the inferred or default language/extension.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. Array of tag strings for the snippet.
Optional. Short description stored in the index.
Optional switch. Overwrites an existing snippet with the same name without prompting.
Optional (Pipe parameter set). Accepts string lines from the pipeline. All lines are collected and joined before saving.
Optional. Overrides the inferred or default language/extension.
Mandatory. The identifier for the new snippet (no spaces, no extension).
Optional (File parameter set). Path to the source file to import. The language is derived from the file extension when -Language is omitted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. Array of tag strings for the snippet.

dev:type.maml:name: Accepts string lines via the pipeline when using the Pipe parameter set.
None. Writes a confirmation message to the host.

maml:alert.maml:para: The Pipe parameter set collects all input in the process block and writes the snippet only in the end block after the pipeline has been fully read.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports deploy.ps1 as a snippet named 'my-script'.
Pipes a Python file's contents into a new snippet named 'parser'.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies a snippet's full content to the Windows clipboard.

Retrieves the content of the named snippet via Show-Snip -PassThru and passes it to Set-Clipboard. The function writes a confirmation message if successful and does nothing if the snippet is not found (Show-Snip handles the error message in that case).
Mandatory. The name of the snippet whose content should be copied.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Mandatory. The name of the snippet whose content should be copied.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes a confirmation message to the host on success.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires a Windows clipboard (Set-Clipboard). In headless or SSH sessions where the clipboard is unavailable, Set-Clipboard may throw; the error is not suppressed.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Copies the content of 'my-snippet' to the clipboard.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Quickly grab a snippet to paste into a terminal Copy-Snip azure-login # then Ctrl+V in any application

maml:description.maml:para: Opens a snippet file in the configured editor and updates its modified timestamp.

Resolves the snippet file path, launches the configured editor (or an override), and waits for the editor process to exit. After the editor closes, the snippet's 'modified' timestamp in index.json is updated to the current UTC time. The editor resolution order is: -Editor override → configured Editor → EditorFallbacks list (nvim, code, notepad) → notepad as the final fallback.
Mandatory. The name of the snippet to edit.
Optional. Overrides the configured editor for this invocation only. Must be a command resolvable on PATH (e.g., 'code', 'nvim', 'notepad').
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. Overrides the configured editor for this invocation only. Must be a command resolvable on PATH (e.g., 'code', 'nvim', 'notepad').
Mandatory. The name of the snippet to edit.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. The editor runs synchronously; control returns after the editor exits.

maml:alert.maml:para: The function calls script:GetEditor which walks the Editor and EditorFallbacks configuration keys. The @($cfg.Editor) wrapping ensures the Editor value is always iterated as an array even when stored as a bare string in the config.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens 'my-snippet' in the default configured editor.
Opens 'my-snippet' in Visual Studio Code regardless of the configured editor.

maml:description.maml:para: Exports a local snippet to GitHub as a new or updated Gist.

Reads the snippet file and its metadata, then creates a new GitHub Gist via POST or updates the existing linked Gist via PATCH. The decision is based on whether the snippet's 'gistId' field in the index is set. After a successful API call, the Gist ID and URL are written back to index.json so that future calls update the same Gist. New Gists are secret by default; use -Public to create a publicly visible Gist.
Mandatory. The name of the local snippet to export.
Optional. A description for the Gist. Falls back to the snippet's description, then the snippet name if not provided.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional switch. Creates a public Gist. Default is a secret Gist.
Optional. A description for the Gist. Falls back to the snippet's description, then the snippet name if not provided.
Mandatory. The name of the local snippet to export.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional switch. Creates a public Gist. Default is a secret Gist.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes the resulting Gist URL to the host on success.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires a GitHub PAT with the 'gist' scope. If the snippet has a gistId in the index, the Gist is updated (PATCH). If not, a new Gist is created (POST) and the ID is saved to the index.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a secret Gist from 'my-snippet' or updates the linked one.
Creates or updates a public Gist with a specific description.

maml:description.maml:para: Displays the full content of a GitHub Gist including all its files.

Fetches a specific Gist from the GitHub API by ID and prints each file's content to the terminal with syntax-coloured headers. If a file is marked truncated in the API response, the raw_url is fetched separately to retrieve the full content. Returns the raw Gist API object for pipeline use.
Mandatory. The GitHub Gist ID (32-character hex string) to retrieve.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Mandatory. The GitHub Gist ID (32-character hex string) to retrieve.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
Returns the deserialized Gist object from the GitHub API containing id,
description, html_url, files, owner, and related metadata.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires a GitHub PAT with the 'gist' scope. Truncated file content (>1 MB) is fetched via an additional web request to the file's raw_url.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Fetches and displays all files in the specified Gist.
Retrieves the Gist object and accesses its HTML URL.

maml:description.maml:para: Lists GitHub Gists for the authenticated user or a specified GitHub username.

Calls the GitHub Gists API to retrieve a list of Gists and displays them in a formatted table showing the Gist ID, description, and file names. The number of results is controlled by -Count (default 30, max 100 per API call). Use -Filter to restrict results to Gists whose description or file names contain the given substring. Returns the raw API response objects for pipeline use.
Optional. A substring to match against Gist descriptions and file names. Case-insensitive.
Optional. Maximum number of Gists to retrieve per API request. Default: 30.
Optional. Retrieve Gists for a different GitHub user. When omitted, defaults to the configured GitHubUsername, or the authenticated user's Gists.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. Maximum number of Gists to retrieve per API request. Default: 30.
Optional. A substring to match against Gist descriptions and file names. Case-insensitive.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. Retrieve Gists for a different GitHub user. When omitted, defaults to the configured GitHubUsername, or the authenticated user's Gists.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
Returns the deserialized Gist API response objects. Each object contains id,
description, html_url, files, and other GitHub API fields.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires a GitHub PAT with the 'gist' scope. Set via: Set-SnipConfig -GitHubToken 'ghp_...' or $env:GITHUB_TOKEN

dev:remarks.maml:para: Lists the 30 most recent Gists for the configured user.
Lists up to 50 Gists whose description or file name contains 'deploy'.
Lists public Gists for the GitHub user 'octocat'.

maml:description.maml:para: Lists local snippets with optional filtering by name, description, tag, or language.

Reads the snippet index (index.json) and outputs a formatted table of all matching snippets. Filtering is case-insensitive and matches against the snippet name, its description, and its tags when -Filter is used. Use -Tag for an exact tag match or -Language to restrict by file extension. Returns an array of PSCustomObject rows so results can be piped to other commands. The result set is always wrapped in @() to guarantee an array is returned even when only a single snippet matches.
Optional. A wildcard substring matched against the snippet name, description, and tags. Accepts partial strings (e.g., 'azure' matches 'azure-deploy').
Optional. A file extension (without the dot) to restrict results to a single language (e.g., 'py', 'ps1', 'js').
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. An exact tag value to filter by. The snippet must have this tag in its tags array to be included in the results.
Optional. A wildcard substring matched against the snippet name, description, and tags. Accepts partial strings (e.g., 'azure' matches 'azure-deploy').
Optional. A file extension (without the dot) to restrict results to a single language (e.g., 'py', 'ps1', 'js').
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. An exact tag value to filter by. The snippet must have this tag in its tags array to be included in the results.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
Each object has Name, Lang, Gist, Tags, Modified, and Desc properties.
Returns nothing (displays an info message) when no snippets match.

maml:alert.maml:para: The @($m.tags) wrapping inside the filter logic normalises tags to an array even when the JSON deserializer returns a bare string for a single-element array (a known PowerShell 5.1 ConvertFrom-Json quirk).

dev:remarks.maml:para: Lists all snippets in the index.
Lists all snippets whose name, description, or tags contain 'azure'.
Lists PowerShell snippets tagged 'devops'.

maml:description.maml:para: Shows the current PSSnips configuration settings.

Reads and displays all settings from the PSSnips config.json file located in the ~/.pssnips directory. Settings include the editor command, GitHub token and username, snippet storage path, default language, and delete confirmation preference. GitHub tokens are masked in the output, showing only the last four characters.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Output is written directly to the host (formatted table).

maml:alert.maml:para: Configuration is stored as JSON at ~/.pssnips/config.json. Use Set-SnipConfig to change individual settings.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays the full configuration table in the terminal.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Check which editor is configured before editing a snippet Get-SnipConfig Edit-Snip my-deploy-script

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads a GitHub Gist and saves it as one or more local snippets.

Fetches the specified Gist from GitHub and writes each selected file to the configured SnippetsDir. The snippet language is inferred from the file extension. Multi-file Gists prompt interactively for which file to import unless -All is specified. If a snippet with the derived name already exists, a numeric suffix is appended to avoid collision (unless -Force is used). The Gist ID and URL are stored in the snippet's index metadata to enable future sync operations.
Mandatory. The GitHub Gist ID to import.
Optional switch. Imports all files from the Gist as separate snippets.
Optional. Imports only the specified file from a multi-file Gist.
Optional switch. Overwrites existing snippets with the same name.
Optional. Override the local snippet name. Only applies when importing a single file; ignored when -All is used.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional switch. Imports all files from the Gist as separate snippets.
Optional. Imports only the specified file from a multi-file Gist.
Optional switch. Overwrites existing snippets with the same name.
Mandatory. The GitHub Gist ID to import.
Optional. Override the local snippet name. Only applies when importing a single file; ignored when -All is used.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes a confirmation message per imported snippet.

maml:alert.maml:para: When -Name is not supplied, the snippet name is derived from the Gist file name (without extension). For multi-file imports with -All, each file is stored using its original file base name.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports the first (or only) file from the Gist as a local snippet.
Imports the Gist and saves it with the local name 'my-local-name'.
Imports every file in the Gist as individual snippets.

maml:description.maml:para: Initializes the PSSnips data directory and writes the default configuration.

Creates the ~/.pssnips directory and its snippets subdirectory if they do not exist. Writes a default config.json and an empty index.json when those files are absent. Displays the detected editor and reminds the user to configure a GitHub token if Gist features are needed. This function is called automatically when the module is imported; calling it manually is useful after a fresh installation or to repair a missing configuration.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes status messages to the host.

maml:alert.maml:para: The module calls script:InitEnv automatically on import, so explicit calls to Initialize-PSSnips are typically not needed in normal usage. Data directory: ~/.pssnips (controlled by $script:Home)

dev:remarks.maml:para: Ensures the data directory and config files exist and reports the ready state.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: # Re-initialise after manually deleting the config directory Remove-Item ~/.pssnips -Recurse -Force Initialize-PSSnips

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads and executes a GitHub Gist file without saving it locally.

Fetches a Gist from GitHub, writes the selected file to a temporary path in $env:TEMP, executes it with the appropriate language runner, and then deletes the temporary file in a finally block. The runner selection follows the same logic as Invoke-Snip (ps1, py, js, bat/cmd, sh, rb, go). Supports -WhatIf via ShouldProcess - with -WhatIf the file is not written or executed. When the Gist has multiple files, the first file matching a known runnable extension is selected automatically; use -FileName to specify explicitly.
Mandatory. The GitHub Gist ID to fetch and run.
Optional. Arguments forwarded to the language runner after the file path.
Optional. The specific file within the Gist to run. When omitted, the first file with a known runnable extension is selected.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Optional. Arguments forwarded to the language runner after the file path.
Optional. The specific file within the Gist to run. When omitted, the first file with a known runnable extension is selected.
Mandatory. The GitHub Gist ID to fetch and run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
Variable. Output depends on the language runner.

maml:alert.maml:para: The temporary file is always deleted after execution (or on error) via a try/finally block. The temp file is placed in $env:TEMP with a random name. Requires a GitHub PAT with the 'gist' scope.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Fetches and executes the runnable file in the specified Gist.
Runs the named file from the Gist.
Shows what would be executed without actually running it.

maml:description.maml:para: {{ Fill in the Synopsis }}
{{ Fill in the Description }}
Name of the snippet to run
{{ Fill ArgumentList Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ArgumentList Description }}
Name of the snippet to run
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> {{ Add example code here }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: {{ Add example description here }}

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new snippet file and opens it in the configured editor.

Creates a snippet file in the configured snippets directory, registers it in the index (index.json), and opens the file in the editor unless -Content is provided. When -Content is supplied the file is written with that content and the editor is not launched. When no -Content is given, a language-appropriate template is written first. If a snippet with the same name already exists the function warns and returns without overwriting.
Mandatory. A short identifier for the snippet (no spaces, no extension). Used as both the file base name and the index key.

Optional. The file extension (without dot) that determines the snippet's language and runner (e.g., 'ps1', 'py', 'js', 'bat', 'sh', 'rb', 'go'). Defaults to the configured DefaultLanguage (ps1 out of the box).
Optional. If provided, this string is written directly to the snippet file and the editor is not launched. Useful for programmatic creation.
Optional. A short human-readable description stored in the index and shown in Get-Snip listings.
Optional. Overrides the configured editor for this single invocation (e.g., 'code' to open in VS Code).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. An array of tag strings to categorise the snippet (e.g., @('devops', 'azure')).
Optional. If provided, this string is written directly to the snippet file and the editor is not launched. Useful for programmatic creation.
Optional. A short human-readable description stored in the index and shown in Get-Snip listings.
Optional. Overrides the configured editor for this single invocation (e.g., 'code' to open in VS Code).

Optional. The file extension (without dot) that determines the snippet's language and runner (e.g., 'ps1', 'py', 'js', 'bat', 'sh', 'rb', 'go'). Defaults to the configured DefaultLanguage (ps1 out of the box).
Mandatory. A short identifier for the snippet (no spaces, no extension). Used as both the file base name and the index key.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. An array of tag strings to categorise the snippet (e.g., @('devops', 'azure')).

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes a success or warning message to the host.

maml:alert.maml:para: Template files for ps1, py, js, ts, bat, sh, rb, and go are automatically populated with the snippet name and description as header comments. The editor is determined by script:GetEditor which walks the configured Editor then EditorFallbacks list.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new PowerShell snippet and opens it in the default editor.
Creates a Python snippet tagged 'data' and 'util'.
Creates a snippet with pre-filled content without opening an editor.

maml:description.maml:para: Deletes a local snippet file and removes its index entry.

Looks up the snippet by name in the index, optionally prompts for confirmation (based on the ConfirmDelete config setting or the -Force switch), deletes the snippet file from disk, and removes the metadata entry from index.json. If the snippet name is not found the function displays an error and returns without throwing.
Mandatory. The name of the snippet to delete.
Optional switch. Bypasses the interactive confirmation prompt regardless of the ConfirmDelete configuration setting.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Optional switch. Bypasses the interactive confirmation prompt regardless of the ConfirmDelete configuration setting.
Mandatory. The name of the snippet to delete.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes a success or error message to the host.

maml:alert.maml:para: The physical file and the index entry are both removed. This action is not reversible. Linked GitHub Gists are not affected.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Deletes 'old-script', prompting for confirmation if ConfirmDelete is $true.
Deletes 'old-script' immediately without any confirmation prompt.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates one or more PSSnips configuration settings.

Loads the current configuration from ~/.pssnips/config.json, applies any provided parameter values, and saves the updated configuration back to disk. Only the parameters you supply are changed; unspecified settings retain their current values. Multiple settings can be updated in a single call.
The command name or path of the preferred text editor (e.g., 'edit', 'nvim', 'code'). Optional. Falls back through EditorFallbacks if the command is not found on PATH.
A GitHub personal access token (PAT) with the 'gist' scope. Optional. Required for all Gist operations. Can also be supplied via $env:GITHUB_TOKEN.
Your GitHub username. Optional. Used to list your own Gists when calling Get-GistList without specifying -Username.
Absolute path to the directory where snippet files are stored. Optional. Defaults to ~/.pssnips/snippets. The directory is created if it does not exist.
The file extension (without dot) used when creating a new snippet without an explicit -Language parameter (e.g., 'ps1', 'py', 'js'). Optional.
When $true (the default), Remove-Snip prompts for confirmation before deleting. Set to $false to suppress the confirmation prompt globally. Optional.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
When $true (the default), Remove-Snip prompts for confirmation before deleting. Set to $false to suppress the confirmation prompt globally. Optional.
The file extension (without dot) used when creating a new snippet without an explicit -Language parameter (e.g., 'ps1', 'py', 'js'). Optional.
The command name or path of the preferred text editor (e.g., 'edit', 'nvim', 'code'). Optional. Falls back through EditorFallbacks if the command is not found on PATH.
A GitHub personal access token (PAT) with the 'gist' scope. Optional. Required for all Gist operations. Can also be supplied via $env:GITHUB_TOKEN.
Your GitHub username. Optional. Used to list your own Gists when calling Get-GistList without specifying -Username.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Absolute path to the directory where snippet files are stored. Optional. Defaults to ~/.pssnips/snippets. The directory is created if it does not exist.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes a confirmation message to the host on success.

maml:alert.maml:para: Settings are persisted to ~/.pssnips/config.json as UTF-8 JSON. GitHub tokens are stored in plain text; consider using $env:GITHUB_TOKEN as an alternative for improved security.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Switches the default editor to Neovim.
Saves GitHub credentials to enable Gist integration.
Sets Python as the default language and disables delete confirmation prompts.

maml:description.maml:para: Manages the tags on a local snippet: replace, add, or remove individual tags.

Loads the snippet's current tags from index.json and applies one of three mutations depending on the parameters provided: -Tags Replaces all existing tags with the supplied array. -Add Appends each supplied tag that is not already present (no duplicates). -Remove Removes each supplied tag from the current tag list. The updated tag list is saved back to index.json and the snippet's 'modified' timestamp is refreshed. Tags are stored as a string array in the index.
Mandatory. The name of the snippet to update.
Optional. Tags to append to the existing set. Duplicates are silently ignored.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. Tags to remove from the existing set. Tags not present are ignored.
Optional. Replaces the snippet's entire tag set with these values.
Optional. Tags to append to the existing set. Duplicates are silently ignored.
Mandatory. The name of the snippet to update.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional. Tags to remove from the existing set. Tags not present are ignored.
Optional. Replaces the snippet's entire tag set with these values.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes a confirmation message listing the updated tags.

maml:alert.maml:para: Tags are normalised through [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]] to guarantee correct array serialisation in JSON, avoiding the PS 5.1 single-element array-to-string collapse that can occur with ConvertTo-Json.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Replaces all tags on 'my-snippet' with 'devops' and 'azure'.
Appends the tag 'cloud' to the existing tags without removing any.
Removes the tag 'old-tag' while keeping all other tags intact.

maml:description.maml:para: Displays the content of a named snippet in the terminal.

Reads the snippet file from disk and writes its content to the terminal. By default, a decorative header showing the snippet name, description, and Gist URL (if linked) is printed before the content. Use -Raw to suppress the header and print only the raw file contents. Use -PassThru to return the content as a string for use in scripts or pipelines instead of printing it.
Mandatory. The name of the snippet to display (without file extension).
Optional switch. When specified, returns the snippet content as a string instead of writing to the host. The decorative header is not printed.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional switch. When specified, suppresses the decorative header and prints only the raw file content.
Mandatory. The name of the snippet to display (without file extension).
Optional switch. When specified, returns the snippet content as a string instead of writing to the host. The decorative header is not printed.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional switch. When specified, suppresses the decorative header and prints only the raw file content.

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
Only when -PassThru is specified. Returns the full file content as a string.
Otherwise outputs nothing (writes directly to the host).

maml:alert.maml:para: If the snippet name is not found in the file system, an error message is displayed and the function returns without throwing.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays the snippet content with a decorative header.
Returns the snippet content as a string and copies it to the clipboard.
Prints the raw file content without any header decoration.

maml:description.maml:para: Launches the full-screen interactive terminal snippet manager (TUI).

Starts a full-screen text UI that displays a navigable list of snippets with real-time search filtering. The UI is drawn using [Console]::SetCursorPosition for in-place redraws without flickering. Navigation uses raw virtual key codes read via $Host.UI.RawUI.ReadKey: VK 38 (Up arrow) - move selection up VK 40 (Down arrow) - move selection down VK 13 (Enter) - open detail view VK 39 (Right arrow) - open detail view VK 27 (Esc) - return to list view from detail VK 37 (Left arrow) - return to list view from detail Single-character commands (n, e, r, c, d, g, /) are handled in the default branch of the key switch. The cursor is hidden during TUI operation and restored in a finally block to ensure visibility is not lost on error.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: None. All interaction is through the console.

maml:alert.maml:para: Requires an interactive host with RawUI support. Will not work correctly in non-interactive sessions (e.g., CI pipelines) or when stdout is redirected. The TUI shows up to 20 snippets per page; use [/] to filter when the collection exceeds 20 items.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Launches the interactive TUI snippet manager.
Equivalent shortcut: calling snip with no arguments starts the TUI.

maml:description.maml:para: Synchronises a local snippet with its linked GitHub Gist (pull or push).

Bi-directional sync between a local snippet and the GitHub Gist it was linked to via Export-Gist or Import-Gist. By default (pull mode) the local snippet file is overwritten with the latest content from GitHub. With -Push, the local file's current content is uploaded to GitHub, updating the Gist. The snippet must already have a linked gistId; run Export-Gist first to establish the link.
Mandatory. The name of the local snippet to synchronise.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional switch. Pushes the local snippet content to GitHub (update Gist). Without this switch, the default is to pull (download from GitHub).
Mandatory. The name of the local snippet to synchronise.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Optional switch. Pushes the local snippet content to GitHub (update Gist). Without this switch, the default is to pull (download from GitHub).

dev:type.maml:name: None. This function does not accept pipeline input.
None. Writes a status message to the host.

maml:alert.maml:para: Pull mode calls Import-Gist -Force which overwrites the local file. Push mode calls Export-Gist which PATCHes the existing Gist. The snippet must have a non-null gistId in the index. If not, an error is displayed directing the user to run Export-Gist first.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Pulls the latest Gist content from GitHub into the local snippet file.
Uploads the current local snippet content to the linked GitHub Gist.
